paper.title: Phosphorylation-dependent modulation of the Lamin A/C–EZH2 complex regulates epithelial–mesenchymal plasticity

entities.name: Phosphorylation-dependent modulation of the Lamin A/C–EZH2 complex regulates epithelial–mesenchymal plasticity

evidence_entities.text: CDK1-mediated phosphorylation of Lamin A/C (Ser22) and EZH2 (Thr345) disrupts Lamin A/C–EZH2 interaction, destabilizing EZH2, with a concomitant decrease in the occupancy of the heterochromatin mark (H3K27me3) on the SNAI1, TWIST1, and ZEB1 promoters
phosphodeficient Lamin A/C (S22A) and EZH2 (T345A) mutants restore epithelial identity
xenograft assays in NOD-SCID mice reveal that while phosphorylated Lamin A/C or EZH2 promote tumor growth and metastasis, phospho-deficient mutants markedly suppress it
Lamin A/C showed a progressive decline with increasing mesenchymal characteristics
Lamin A/C depletion showed an increase in the AR of MCF7 cells
